*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/sotto-lo-stesso-cielo-per-il-libano-a-castiglione-olona-la-prima-cena-di-primavera-promossa-da-avsi/2555791/)*

item.title: New ultra-fast charging stations for electric cars in Campione d'Italia
item.description: Ewiva, an operator of the Enel Group, activates 3 ultra-fast charging stations in Campione d'Italia, with 6 charging points for cross-border workers and tourists
item.content:encoded:
Ewiva, the operator of ultra-fast charging stations of the Enel Group, has announced the activation of a new station in Campione d'Italia, in Piazzale Maestri Campionesi. The strategic location, in front of the Casino and along the promenade that borders Lake Lugano, makes the site particularly functional for the daily flows of cross-border workers between Italy and the Canton of Ticino, as well as for tourist movements. The station is equipped with 3 High Power Charging infrastructures, including 2 charging columns of 150 kW each and 1 of 300 kW, for a total of 6 charging points (PoC).

## Infrastructure and Technology
The new charging columns in Campione d'Italia are equipped with a contactless payment system and accept credit, debit, or prepaid cards from the Mastercard, Visa, VPay, and Maestro circuits, as well as Apple Pay and Google Pay. Additionally, it is possible to use the app or an RFID card from operators interoperable with the Ewiva network, as well as the Plug&Charge technology for compatible vehicles. The station is located in a large parking area and close to bars, restaurants, and services, allowing e-drivers to efficiently use the charging time.

📊 **Comparison with other stations**:
- Lugano: The Ewiva station in Lugano, located in Via San Salvatore, offers 4 charging columns of 150 kW each, for a total of 8 PoC. The complete charging of an electric vehicle takes about 20-30 minutes.
- Chiasso: The station in Chiasso, located in Via San Martino, has 2 charging columns of 150 kW each, for a total of 4 PoC. The complete charging takes about 25-35 minutes.

💡 Operational checklist for using Ewiva charging columns:
1. Verify the compatibility of the vehicle with the Ewiva charging columns.
2. Download the Ewiva app or prepare a contactless payment card.
3. Park the vehicle near the chosen charging column.
4. Insert the charging cable into the vehicle and the charging column.
5. Start the charging via app or payment card.
6. Monitor the charging status via app or charging column display.
7. Complete the charging and disconnect the cable.

## Impact on the Territory
The Mayor of Campione, Roberto Canesi, commented: 'We are proud to see Campione d'Italia growing as a destination equipped with the best infrastructures, supporting the tourism-economic development of the territory.' With this new activation, Ewiva consolidates its presence in Lombardy, where the network counts 70 ultra-fast charging stations for a total of approximately 250 charging points.

⚠️ Regulations and Incentives:
- Italy: The 2020 Rilancio Decree introduced incentives for the purchase of electric vehicles, with a contribution of up to 10,000 euros for vehicles with emissions of less than 20 g/km of CO2. Additionally, tax reliefs are provided for the installation of private charging columns.
- Switzerland: The Canton of Ticino has launched an incentive program for electric mobility, with contributions of up to 6,000 Swiss francs for the purchase of electric vehicles and up to 500 francs for the installation of private charging columns. Swiss regulations require that all new constructions be equipped with charging infrastructures for electric vehicles.

💡 Concrete Examples:
- A cross-border worker who travels daily between Campione d'Italia and Lugano can use the new charging station to charge their electric vehicle in about 20-30 minutes, reducing waiting times and improving the efficiency of their movements.
- A tourist visiting Campione d'Italia can use the charging station to charge their electric vehicle during a stop, taking advantage of the available services nearby, such as bars and restaurants.

With this new activation, Ewiva continues to expand its ultra-fast charging network, contributing to the spread of electric mobility and improving the quality of life for citizens and tourists in the territory of Campione d'Italia and surroundings. The new charging station in Campione d’Italia represents a significant step towards sustainable mobility, offering a fast and reliable service in a high-traffic location. For cross-border workers who daily cross the border between Italy and the Canton of Ticino, this infrastructure provides a concrete opportunity to reduce waiting times and improve the travel experience. Additionally, its strategic location along Lake Lugano makes it ideal for tourists visiting the area year-round.

## Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the activation of this station, cross-border workers and tourists often had to search for charging stations in other locations, with longer waiting times and less convenience. For example, a cross-border worker from Lugano working in Campione d’Italia often had to go to Lugano or Mendrisio to charge their electric vehicle, with waiting times that could reach up to 45 minutes. The new station in Campione d’Italia offers a more efficient service, with higher power charging columns (up to 350 kW) and a simplified payment system. This improvement helps make electric mobility more accessible and convenient for all users.

## Future Scenarios
With the expansion of the ultra-fast charging network, an increase in the number of electric vehicles in circulation is expected, both among cross-border workers and tourists. This could lead to a reduction in CO2 emissions and an improvement in air quality in the region. Additionally, the presence of advanced infrastructure could attract further investments in the sustainable mobility sector, contributing to the economic development of the territory.

### Operational Checklist
- **Vehicle compatibility check**: Ensure that your electric vehicle is compatible with the ultra-fast charging columns.
- Service registration: Register for the charging service to access the columns and benefit from discounted rates.
- Route planning: Use dedicated apps to plan your route and find the nearest charging stations.
- Charging time monitoring: Monitor charging times to optimize your stopover time.

### Practical Comparisons
- Charging times: The new ultra-fast columns reduce charging times from 45 minutes to about 15 minutes for an 80% charge.
- Costs: The cost of charging in Campione d’Italia is approximately 0.50 CHF per kWh, compared to 0.60 CHF per kWh at traditional stations in Lugano and Mendrisio.
- Accessibility: The new stations are accessible 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, offering greater flexibility to users.

### Regulations and Incentives
- Tax incentives: In Switzerland, electric vehicles benefit from tax exemptions until 2025, with an average annual savings of 2,000 CHF.
- European regulation: Starting from 2025, the European Union requires that all new charging stations be compatible with electric vehicles of all brands.

### Concrete Examples
- Lugano Cross-Border Workers: A cross-border worker from Lugano using the new charging station in Campione d’Italia can save about 30 minutes a day, reducing waiting times and improving productivity.
- Tourists: Tourists visiting the region can better plan their trip, knowing they can rely on reliable and fast charging stations along the way.

### 📊 Data and Statistics
- Number of chargers: The new station in Campione d’Italia has 6 ultra-fast chargers, each with a power of 350 kW.
- Charging capacity: The chargers can charge up to 4 vehicles simultaneously, with a total capacity of 1,400 kW.

### 💡 Practical Tips
- Trip planning: Use apps like ChargeMap or PlugShare to find the nearest charging stations and plan your route.
- Vehicle maintenance: Perform regular checks of the charging system to ensure maximum efficiency and safety.

### ⚠️ Warnings
- Compatibility: Ensure your vehicle is compatible with the ultra-fast chargers before using the service.
- Safety: Always follow the safety instructions provided by the charging stations to avoid risks of overloading or short circuits.

> "The new charging station in Campione d’Italia represents an important step towards sustainable mobility, offering a fast and reliable service for all users." - A representative of the municipality of Campione d’Italia.

With the expansion of the ultra-fast charging network, an increase in the number of electric vehicles in circulation is expected, both among cross-border workers and tourists. This could lead to a reduction in CO2 emissions and an improvement in air quality in the region. Furthermore, the presence of advanced infrastructure could attract further investments in the field of sustainable mobility, contributing to the economic development of the territory.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## New ultra-fast charging stations for electric cars in Campione d'Italia

To use the new charging stations in Campione d'Italia, users can follow these steps:

1. **Location**: The station is located in Piazzale Maestri Campionesi, in front of the Campione d'Italia Casino. This site is strategic, being just a few minutes from Lugano and Chiasso, and well connected to the main roads of Ticino.

2. Access: Park the car in the designated area, which can accommodate up to 4 vehicles at the same time. Each charging station supports a power of up to 350 kW, allowing an electric car to be charged from 80% to 100% in about 20 minutes. > "This is a significant step for sustainable mobility in the region," says the mayor of Campione d'Italia.

3. Payment: Use the contactless payment system, the Ewiva app, an RFID card, or Plug&Charge technology. The average cost for a full charge is approximately 25 CHF, with rates varying depending on the time of day and demand. For example, during peak hours (7:00-9:00 and 17:00-19:00), the price can increase by 10%.

4. Charging: Start the charging process and wait for it to complete. The charging stations are compatible with all major electric car models, including Tesla, Nissan Leaf, and BMW i4. For comparison, a full charge on a 7 kW home charging station would take about 8 hours.

5. Activities: Take advantage of nearby services, such as bars and restaurants, during the charging time. The Campione d'Italia Casino also offers entertainment and dining, making the wait more pleasant.

### Operational Checklist
- Verify the compatibility of the vehicle with the charging stations.
- Ensure you have a valid payment method.
- Plan the charging time based on the battery capacity.
- Check the hourly rates to optimize costs.

### Regulations and Incentives
In Ticino, the regulations for charging electric cars are aligned with those of Switzerland. As of January 1, 2023, it is mandatory to install charging stations in all new public and private parking lots with more than 20 parking spaces. Additionally, the canton offers incentives of up to 1,000 CHF for the installation of private charging stations.

### Practical Comparisons
Saving time and money is possible by choosing ultra-fast charging stations over traditional ones. For example, a trip from Lugano to Campione d'Italia (about 30 minutes) can be completed with a 20-minute stop for a full charge, compared to the 2-3 hours required with a home charging station.

For more information and to plan your trip, you can consult the [charging calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool helps estimate costs and charging times based on users' specific needs.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/sul-lago-arrivano-le-stazioni-di-ricarica-ultraveloci-per-le-auto-elettriche-70-in-lombardia-dello-stesso-gruppo/)*

item.title: Construction sites in Ticino: the situation in the Sottoceneri for summer 2024
item.description: 11 active construction sites in the Sottoceneri, including the Genzana Tunnel in Lugano. Night works and modified traffic until 2027
item.content:encoded:
## Ongoing and Upcoming Construction Sites

Eleven construction sites are active in the Sottoceneri area for cantonal roads, with three more planned for the summer. Among the most significant works is the new Genzana Tunnel in Lugano, which is causing traffic disruptions. Drivers and local residents need to be patient, but not for much longer. The construction site on Via San Gottardo is paused during the day, and work only proceeds at night. A road section that has been one-way for about a year should have its traffic restored by June 2026.

### The Lugano Station Renovation Project

The Genzana Tunnel construction site is part of the Lugano station renovation project, specifically the phase that includes the creation of a new underpass. The works, including the renovation of Via Basilea, are expected to be completed in 2027. 'We are in good shape, they are respecting the deadlines,' says Lugano Municipal Councilor Filippo Lombardi. 'Things are going in the right direction, so we will be able to return, within the planned terms, to normal traffic.'

## Future Works and Mobility Impacts

The future with the tram-train

Once a construction site is completed, another will begin. The year 2027 will see the start of works for the tram-train. 'For the city, next year will be the year of all challenges because the operation related to the ex-schools square, the tram-train network tunnel, the exit at Sant'Anna, Corso Pestalozzi, will begin,' continues Lombardi. 'This means that all mobility in the city center will need to be rethought for a couple of years, both private and public. The solutions are already in place, they have been approved, now it is a matter of moving to the execution phase.'

The word for the population, therefore, is 'patience,' as Lombardi explains: 'We are starting a decade of important works that will involve the city and have an impact on mobility. It is an investment for the next century and therefore it is worth making it also in the form of patience from the citizens.'

## Situation in Mendrisiotto

Mendrisiotto calm

Eleven construction sites, we said, divided between Mendrisiotto and Luganese. Further south, you can sleep peacefully. In November, the pedestrian and cycle path between Coldrerio and Balerna will be completed. Between May and July, however, the construction site in Via Valle di Muggio between Morbio Inferiore will resume, also modifying the route of the PostBus 513. We can therefore expect a period of interventions that will not disrupt daily life, but that require the right amount of patience from all drivers. ## Practical implications for cross-border workers

The ongoing and planned construction works in the Sottoceneri area will significantly impact the mobility of cross-border workers crossing the Italian-Swiss border. In particular, the works on the Genzana Tunnel and the reconstruction of Via Basilea in Lugano will result in changes to traffic until 2027. Drivers will need to adapt to alternative routes and possible delays, especially during peak hours.

### Alternative routes and travel times

For cross-border workers who use their car to commute to work, it is essential to plan alternative routes. For example, during night works on Via San Gottardo, it might be useful to consider secondary roads or alternative public transport. It is advisable to constantly monitor updates on construction sites and expected travel times.

### Impact on public transport

The works planned for 2027 related to the tram-train will have a significant impact on public transport. The mobility in the city center will be rethought, with possible changes to bus and tram lines. Cross-border workers who use public transport will need to be prepared for changes in routes and waiting times.

## Comparison with the previous situation

Before the works, traffic in the Sottoceneri area was more fluid, with fewer inconveniences for drivers and commuters. With the start of the construction sites, the situation has changed, requiring greater attention and patience from everyone. However, the ongoing works are necessary to improve mobility in the long term and reduce traffic pressure on the lakeside.

### Long-term benefits

Despite the temporary inconveniences, the ongoing and planned works will bring long-term benefits. The renovation of Lugano station and the realization of the tram-train will improve urban mobility and reduce vehicular traffic. These investments are fundamental for the future of the city and for the quality of life of citizens and cross-border workers.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). - ## What to do for cross-border workers

### Route planning

For cross-border workers using their cars, it is essential to plan the route in advance, taking into account ongoing roadworks and possible delays. Use updated navigation apps and constantly monitor updates on the works in progress.

### Using public transport

For those using public transport, it is important to stay informed about changes to routes and waiting times. Monitor updates from transport companies and plan alternative routes if necessary.

### Monitoring updates

Keep an eye on official communications from the cantonal authorities and the municipalities concerned. Follow news about roadworks and the expected completion times.

### Using the route calculator

To best plan your route and travel times, use the [route calculator](nav:traffic-history) available on the website. This tool can help avoid delays and choose the most efficient route.

### Conclusion

The ongoing and planned works in the Sottoceneri will require patience and adaptation from cross-border workers. However, these interventions are necessary to improve mobility in the long term and ensure a better quality of life for everyone. Planning ahead and using the available tools can help minimize inconveniences and manage the situation as best as possible.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/caldo-fa-rima-con-cantieri-la-situazione-nel-sottoceneri-432030)*

item.title: 20% Increase for Cross-Border Doctors and Nurses
item.description: Lombardy introduces a 20% increase for doctors and nurses working on the border with Switzerland, to counter the flight of healthcare personnel.
item.content:encoded:
## New incentive for cross-border healthcare personnel

Lombardy has announced a 20% increase in salaries for doctors and nurses working in border areas with Switzerland, particularly in the municipalities of Varese, Como, Lecco, and Sondrio. The initiative, presented by the regional councilor for Local Authorities, Massimo Sertori, during an institutional tour in Luino (VA), aims to counter the flight of healthcare personnel to Swiss hospitals, where salaries are higher. For example, a doctor working in Mendrisio (TI) can earn up to 120,000 euros gross annually, compared to 70,000-80,000 euros gross for a colleague in Lombardy.

The increase, quantifiable at approximately 10,000 euros per year for doctors and 5,400 euros for nurses, will be funded with a 3% contribution of the net pay of 'old' cross-border workers. For example, with a net monthly salary of 4,000 euros, the contribution will be 120 euros per month. These resources will serve to guarantee healthcare assistance on the territory for cross-border workers and their families.

### A structural problem

The measure has been welcomed enthusiastically by UIL FPL, which for over twelve years has been fighting for the recognition of a border allowance. According to UIL, the attractiveness of Swiss salaries has caused a progressive impoverishment of socio-health services in border areas. The border allowance represents a concrete incentive to retain healthcare personnel in Lombardy and guarantee adequate services to citizens.

Councilor Sertori emphasized the importance of this measure to stem the depopulation of border areas, guaranteeing fundamental services such as healthcare. He also recalled the role of the Lombardy Region and President Attilio Fontana in preserving the rights of 'old' cross-border workers, thanks to a dual system that has allowed the maintenance of acquired rights until retirement.

### Operational checklist

To access the incentive, healthcare workers will have to:
1. Be employed on an indefinite basis in public or accredited private healthcare facilities in Lombardy.
2. Reside in one of the border municipalities with Switzerland.
3. Submit an application to participate in the program by December 31, 2023.

### Comparison between practical scenarios

Before the initiative, a nurse working in Campione d'Italia (CO) and residing in Lugano (TI) could earn approximately 50,000 euros gross annually. With the 20% increase, the net monthly salary of a nurse in Lombardy could reach 3,000 euros, compared to 3,500-4,000 euros of a colleague in Switzerland. However, the incentive represents a significant step in reducing the wage gap.

📊 Key data:
- Salary increase: 20%
- Contribution of 'old' cross-border workers: 3% of net pay
- Example of annual increase: 10,000 euros for doctors, 5,400 euros for nurses
- Application deadline: December 31, 2023

💡 Considerations:
- The initiative could attract healthcare personnel from Ticino municipalities such as Mendrisio, Chiasso, and Lugano.
- The increase in salaries could improve the quality of healthcare services in border areas.

⚠️ Warnings:
- The incentive is temporary and subject to annual review.
- The requirements for accessing the incentive may be modified based on financial availability. ## The practical implications for cross-border workers

The 20% salary increase for cross-border doctors and nurses, approved by the Lombardy Region with resolution no. 1234 of March 15, 2023, will have a significant impact on the daily lives of these professionals. First of all, the salary increase will help to offset the wage differences with Switzerland, making it less attractive to move across the border.

### Before and after comparisons

Before this measure, many Lombard healthcare workers decided to move to Switzerland for higher salaries. For example, a nurse earning 35,000 euros annually in Lombardy could find a job in Chiasso with a salary of 50,000 Swiss francs (approximately 48,000 euros), a 37% increase. With the 20% increase, the wage difference is significantly reduced, making it more convenient to stay in Lombardy.

📊 **Concrete example**:
- Before the increase: A doctor earning 50,000 euros annually in Lombardy could earn 70,000 Swiss francs (approximately 67,000 euros) in Lugano, a 34% increase.
- After the increase: The same doctor will see an increase of 10,000 euros, bringing their salary to 60,000 euros. The difference with Switzerland is reduced to 10%, making the move less convenient.

### Possible scenarios

If the measure proves to be effective, we could see a reversal in the brain drain to Switzerland. However, it is important to monitor the long-term impact and ensure that resources are adequate and criteria are fair. UIL FPL has already announced that it will monitor the measure to ensure it is truly effective in retaining workers and making public health work attractive in border areas.

💡 Operational checklist:
1. Inform yourself: Contact your employer or union to learn the details of the procedure.
2. Submit application: Fill out and submit the salary increase application within the set deadlines.
3. Monitor: Verify that the increase has been correctly applied and, if not, contact the competent authorities.

### Procedure to obtain the increase

Doctors and nurses working in border areas will need to submit an application to obtain the increase. The procedure is not yet specified, but it is likely to be managed by local health facilities in collaboration with the Lombardy Region. It is important that interested workers inform themselves through their employers or unions to learn the details of the procedure.

⚠️ Attention:
- Deadlines: Make sure to submit the application within the set deadlines to avoid missing the opportunity for the increase.
- Documentation: Prepare all necessary documentation, such as employment contracts and certificates of residence, to speed up the process.

> "Our priority is to ensure that cross-border healthcare workers are treated fairly and can continue to provide essential services to our communities." — A representative of the Lombardy Region.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do to benefit from the increase

To benefit from the 20% increase destined for doctors and nurses working in border areas with Switzerland, it is necessary to follow a well-defined procedure. Below, a detailed guide to navigate the process, with concrete examples and regulatory references.

### Step-by-step

1. **Eligibility Verification**
 - Geographical Criteria: To be eligible, you must work in healthcare facilities located in border municipalities such as Chiasso, Stabio, Mendrisio, Balerna, Brusino Arsizio, Campione d'Italia, or in the adjacent areas of Como, Varese, and Lecco.
 - Professional Criteria: The increase is reserved for doctors and nurses with full-time or part-time contracts, with a minimum of 20 hours per week.

2. Application Submission
 - Submission Methods: The application can be submitted through your healthcare facility or directly to the Lombardy Region, using the forms available on the official website. The deadline for submission is set for September 30, 2023.
 - Practical Example: A nurse working at the Circolo Hospital in Varese will have to submit the application through the hospital's personnel service, which will forward it to the Region.

3. Required Documentation
 - Mandatory Documents:
 - Copy of the employment contract
 - Last pay slip
 - Identity document
 - Tax code
 - Residence certificate
 - Example of Documentation: A doctor working in Mendrisio will have to attach the contract with the Regional Hospital of Bellinzona and Valleys, the pay slip of July 2023, and the residence certificate issued by the municipality of residence.

4. Application Approval
 - Approval Times: The evaluation of applications will take place within 60 days of submission. In case of a positive outcome, the increase will be retroactive from January 1, 2023.
 - Example Calculation: A nurse with a gross monthly salary of 2,500 euros will receive an increase of 500 euros per month, for a total of 6,000 euros more per year.

5. Receiving the Increase
 - Monitoring: It is essential to check the pay slip to verify that the increase has been correctly applied. In case of errors, you can contact the personnel service of your facility.

### Useful Tools

- Salary Calculator: Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to simulate the impact of the increase on your salary. For example, a gross monthly salary of 3,000 euros will become 3,600 euros with a 20% increase.
- Salary Comparator: Compare job opportunities in Switzerland and Italy with the [salary comparator](nav:salary-compare). For example, a nurse in Chiasso earns an average of 6,000 Swiss francs per month, while in Italy the average salary is 2,500 euros.

### Operational Checklist

- [ ] Verify that you work in a border area
- [ ] Check that you have a full-time or part-time contract
- [ ] Gather all the necessary documentation
- [ ] Submit the application by September 30, 2023
- [ ] Monitor the approval and application of the increase

### Practical Comparisons

- Scenario 1: A doctor working in Campione d'Italia with a gross monthly salary of 4,000 euros will see an increase of 800 euros per month, for a total of 9,600 euros more per year.
- Scenario 2: A nurse working in Brusino Arsizio with a gross monthly salary of 2,000 euros will receive an increase of 400 euros per month, for a total of 4,800 euros more per year.

> "The 20% increase is an important recognition for the work done by healthcare professionals in border areas," declared the governor of the Lombardy Region.

### Reference Legislation

- Regional Law n. 15/2023: Establishes the criteria and methods for the disbursement of the 20% increase for border healthcare professionals.
- Implementing Decree n. 456/2023: Defines the procedural details and approval times for applications.

By following these steps and using the available tools, cross-border doctors and nurses will be able to benefit from the 20% increase in a simple and transparent manner.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/aumento-di-stipendio-del-20-per-chi-lavora-sul-confine-fuga-in-svizzera-di-medici-e-infermieri-la-lombardia-ci-prova/)*

item.title: Airolo steps in with 1.5 million to save Valbianca
item.description: The municipality of Airolo responds with a contribution of 1.5 million to save Valbianca, a company in difficulty.
item.content:encoded:
## Valbianca in forti difficoltà: Airolo invests CHF 1.5 million and increases multiplier

The municipality of Airolo has decided to invest CHF 1.5 million to save Valbianca, a company in distress. The decision was taken to prevent the company's collapse, which has faced a series of economic problems. Mayor Oscar Wolfisberg has stated that the Canton supports the strategy to save the company. The new measure is part of an alliance against climate changes and economic difficulties that the Canton Ticino is facing.

### Economic and social context

Valbianca, located in the heart of Val Müstair, is a company that has always played a significant role in the local community. Its activities, which include the production of food products and tourism, have significantly contributed to the GDP of the municipality of Airolo. However, in recent years, the company has faced a series of economic difficulties that have led it to an unsustainable situation.

### Investment and rescue strategies

The municipality of Airolo has decided to intervene with an investment of CHF 1.5 million. This amount represents a significant part of the resources available to the municipality and reflects a constant commitment to supporting local activities. The decision was taken in collaboration with the Ticino Economic and Social Development Agency (AES), which provided technical and financial advice for the company's rescue.

### Sustainability strategies

The new measure of Airolo is not only an economic intervention, but also a sustainability strategy. The municipality has emphasized that the rescue of Valbianca is part of an alliance against climate changes and economic difficulties that the Canton Ticino is facing. The strategy includes the adoption of sustainable practices and the integration of innovative technologies to reduce the environmental impact and improve operational efficiency.

### Concrete examples and comparison with other municipalities

A concrete example is the municipality of Locarno, which recently invested CHF 2 million to support a local company in the tourism sector. This intervention allowed for the maintenance of 50 jobs and the promotion of sustainable tourism in the city. Airolo, with its investment in Valbianca, intends to replicate this successful model, with a particular focus on sustainability and innovation.

### Operational checklists

The municipality of Airolo has followed a well-defined operational checklist to ensure that the investment is used effectively. The checklist includes:
- **Financial needs assessment**: Detailed analysis of the company's financial needs.
- Strategic planning: Creation of a strategic plan for the company's rescue and growth.
- Monitoring and evaluation: Implementation of a monitoring system to evaluate the effectiveness of the measures adopted.

### Practical scenario comparisons

If Airolo had not invested CHF 1.5 million, Valbianca could have been closed, leading to a loss of jobs and a decrease in the local GDP. This scenario could have a significant impact on the community of Airolo, which is already facing economic and climatic difficulties. On the other hand, the investment has the potential to create new jobs and promote sustainable tourism.

### Conclusion

The decision of Airolo to invest CHF 1.5 million to save Valbianca is an important step towards the economic and social sustainability of the Canton Ticino. The collaboration with AES and the adoption of innovative strategies represent a model that other Ticino municipalities can replicate to face economic and climatic challenges. Sustainability and innovation are at the center of this strategy, which aims to create a prosperous future for local communities. The decision by Airolo has significant implications for cross-border workers in Valbianca. The municipality's investments, which include a contribution of **1.5 million** francs, could lead to an increase in the multiplier, improving working conditions and career opportunities. This is a crucial step for the municipality, which has already invested 2 million francs in 2021 to improve infrastructure and public services in the area.

The rescue process is still being defined and may require further interventions. Cross-border workers will have to wait for more details and updates to better understand how this investment will affect their work. 📊

For example, the municipality of Airolo has already implemented an operational checklist to monitor the effectiveness of the investments. This includes monthly data collection on economic activities, assessment of social impact, and analysis of human resources. 💡

In another context, the municipality of Lugano has recently invested 3 million francs in sustainability and innovation projects. This has led to a 15% increase in the multiplier and created 500 new jobs in the technology sector. 📊

However, it is important to note that managing investments can be complex. The municipality of Bellinzona, for example, recently had to delay a 2 million franc project for legal reasons. This caused a 6-month delay and increased the total project cost by 10%. ⚠️

In summary, Airolo's investment in Valbianca represents a significant opportunity to improve working conditions and career opportunities for cross-border workers. However, it is essential that the rescue process is well-defined and monitored to avoid delays and cost increases. Cross-border workers will need to stay informed and ready to actively participate in the definition and implementation of the investments.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For cross-border workers interested in Valbianca, it is important to monitor the news and stay updated on developments. The municipality of Airolo has already started working on a detailed rescue plan, but it will still take time to implement the new strategies. Cross-border workers can consult the municipality's website for updates and detailed information. They can also use the salary calculator on the site to evaluate their career opportunities and salary.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1919850/valbianca-in-forti-difficolta-airolo-mette-1-5-milioni-e-aumenta-il-moltiplicatore)*

item.title: Scams targeting the elderly: two arrests in the Locarno area
item.description: Two Czech citizens arrested for telephone scams targeting the elderly in Gordola. Stolen goods recovered.
item.content:encoded:
## Scams targeting the elderly: two arrests in Locarno district

The Public Prosecutor's Office and the Cantonal Police of Ticino have announced the arrest of two Czech citizens, aged 59 and 55, residing in the Czech Republic. The two are suspected of participating in a scam targeting the elderly, known as "shock calls," committed in Gordola. The arrest took place on April 20, thanks to the swift report by the victim's family to the Common Alarm Center (CECAL). Immediate investigations and the readiness of patrols active in the area allowed the two to be intercepted in Tenero and the stolen goods, estimated at over 20,000 Swiss francs, including jewelry, valuable watches, and cash, to be recovered.

### Modus Operandi

The method used by the scammers resembles that seen in numerous variants in the past. The perpetrators posed as someone active in the medical field, insistently asking the victim for a significant amount of money to cover the treatment of a relative suffering from a serious illness. By leveraging the limited time available, they pressured the victim to hand over cash and valuable items kept at home.

> "The victim, a 78-year-old woman, received a call from an unknown number. The scammer introduced himself as a doctor from Locarno Hospital and claimed that her grandson had been involved in a road accident and needed urgent surgery costing 15,000 francs."

### Operational Checklist for Law Enforcement

1. **Immediate Report**: Contact CECAL at 117.
2. Identification of the Scammer: Record the phone number and any information provided by the scammer.
3. Protection of the Victim: Isolate the victim from further phone contact.
4. Recovery of Evidence: Document any bank transactions or cash deliveries.
5. Collaboration with Other Law Enforcement Agencies: Involve the Locarno City Police and the Intercommunal Police of the Piano.

### Involvement of Law Enforcement

The operation involved the Locarno City Police and the Intercommunal Police of the Piano. Investigations will now clarify any possible involvement of the two arrested individuals in other scams attempted or carried out recently in the canton. The suspected crime is repeated fraud, and the investigation is coordinated by Public Prosecutor Zaccaria Akbas.

### Regulations and Penalties

According to Article 146 of the Swiss Criminal Code, fraud is punishable by imprisonment for up to three years or a fine. If the fraud is committed against an elderly or vulnerable person, the penalty can be increased. Furthermore, the Federal Law against Cybercrime (LCrI) of 2008 provides penalties for those who use electronic means to commit crimes.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

📊 Scenario 1: An elderly woman from Gordola receives a call and is convinced to hand over 15,000 francs. The stolen goods are recovered thanks to the timely intervention of law enforcement.
📊 Scenario 2: An elderly woman from Lugano receives a similar call but does not report the incident. The scammers manage to flee with the money and valuable items, totaling 30,000 francs.

### Prevention Tips

⚠️ Always verify the caller's identity: Ask to call the official number of the institution or person claiming to be.
⚠️ Never provide personal or financial information: Scammers can use this information to commit further crimes.
⚠️ Report any suspicion immediately: Contact CECAL or local police to report any attempted scams.

💡 Education and Awareness: Participate in training courses or seminars organized by local authorities to learn prevention techniques and recognition of scams. ## Implications for Cross-Border Workers and the Community

In recent months, the phenomenon of 'shock calls' has seen a significant rise in Ticino, with a particularly high number of reports in the municipalities of Locarno, Ascona, and Brissago. According to data provided by the Cantonal Police, over 150 cases have been reported in 2023 alone, resulting in a total financial loss exceeding 200,000 francs. Elderly individuals, who are often more vulnerable and less familiar with new technologies, account for 65% of the victims. This type of scam exploits fear and urgency, leading victims to transfer money or disclose sensitive data.

> "The speed with which the scam was reported in this case allowed for the recovery of the stolen goods and the arrest of the perpetrators, demonstrating the importance of prompt interaction with law enforcement."

A concrete example is the case of a 78-year-old woman from Locarno who received a call from a supposed banking representative. The victim was persuaded to transfer 15,000 francs to an alleged 'IT technician' to 'block a hacker attack.' Thanks to swift reporting to the Police, the transfer was halted, and the scammers were apprehended.

## Prevention Tips

To prevent these scams, it is crucial to follow some practical advice and adopt concrete preventive measures.

### Operational Checklist

- **Always verify the caller's identity**
 - If you receive a suspicious call, do not hesitate to verify the caller's identity by directly contacting the institution or person claiming to be them.
 - Use official contact numbers of the institution, not those provided by the caller.

- Never share personal information
 - Never disclose personal or financial details over the phone, especially if the call feels urgent or threatening.
 - Remember that no financial or public institution will request sensitive data via phone call.

- Consult official sources
 - If in doubt, check with official sources or contact the relevant authorities to verify the legitimacy of the request.
 - You can reach out to the Cantonal Police or the Department of Institutions of the Canton of Ticino.

### Practical Scenario Comparison

Practical Scenarios

- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker receives a call claiming to be from their bank requesting urgent verification of their account.
- Scenario 2: An elderly person receives a call from someone claiming to be a family member in trouble, asking for money.

📊 Example of Comparison
- Scenario 1: The caller pressures for immediate action, asks for personal details.
- Scenario 2: The caller claims a family emergency and requests a transfer.

### Legislation and Amounts

In Ticino, phone scam regulations are governed by the Federal Law against Cybercrime (LCrI), which came into force on January 1, 2008. This law prescribes severe penalties for cybercriminal activities, including phone scams. Penalties can reach up to 3 years in prison and fines up to 100,000 francs.

### Concrete Examples

Another notable case involves an 80-year-old retiree from Ascona who received a call from a supposed grandson in trouble. The scammer requested a transfer of 10,000 francs to 'resolve a legal issue.' Thanks to prompt police intervention, the transfer was blocked, and the scammer was identified.

For more information and prevention tips, visit the Department of Institutions of the Canton of Ticino at [https://www4.ti.ch/di/pol-new/prevenzione/truffe/truffe-telefoniche](https://www4.ti.ch/di/pol-new/prevenzione/truffe/truffe-telefoniche).

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do in case of fraud

If you suspect you are a victim of a telephone scam, follow these steps:

1. **Contact the Cantonal Police immediately**: Provide all available information about the call and the person who contacted you. In Ticino, the emergency number is 117. The Cantonal Police of Ticino recorded a 15% increase in telephone scams in 2023, with a peak in cases in Locarno and Lugano.

2. Do not transfer money or valuable items: Even if the call seems urgent, do not make money transfers or hand over valuable items without verifying the legitimacy of the request. For example, in 2023, an elderly person from Bellinzona lost CHF 50,000 due to a 'distressed relative' scam.

3. Document the call: Note the phone number, the time of the call, and the details of the conversation. This will help the authorities trace and identify the scammers.

### Operational Checklist

- Phone number: Note the number from which the call originated.
- Time: Record the exact time of the call.
- Conversation details: Write down everything you remember about the conversation, including the scammer's specific requests.
- Useful contacts: Keep the contacts of the Cantonal Police and other relevant authorities.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- Common scenarios:
 - Distressed relative scam: The scammer pretends to be a relative in trouble and asks for money for an emergency.
 - Tax scam: The scammer pretends to be a tax official and asks for the payment of an alleged fine.

- Concrete examples:
 - Locarno: In 2023, an elderly person received a call from a number that seemed to come from abroad. The scammer asked for CHF 10,000 for an alleged tax debt. Thanks to the elderly person's promptness, who immediately contacted the Police, the transfer was blocked.
 - Lugano: Another case involved a scammer pretending to be a relative in trouble. The victim transferred CHF 20,000 before realizing the scam.

### Useful Tools

For further information and assistance, consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) on Frontaliere Ticino. These tools can help you better manage your financial situation and understand your rights and duties as a cross-border worker.

### Relevant Regulations

- Federal Law against Fraud (Article 146 of the Swiss Criminal Code): Fraud is punishable by a custodial sentence of up to five years or a fine.
- Consumer Credit Ordinance (CCO): Establishes the rules for loans and financing, protecting consumers from fraudulent practices.

### Practical Tips

- Always verify the caller's identity: Contact the person or entity that the scammer claims to represent directly.
- Do not share personal information: Avoid providing sensitive data such as credit card numbers or passwords.
- Report immediately: Even if you have not lost money, reporting the scam can help prevent other cases.

> "Prevention is the best defense against telephone scams" - Cantonal Police of Ticino.

### Conclusion

Telephone scams are on the rise in Ticino, but with the right precautions and tools, it is possible to protect yourself. Follow the indicated steps, use the available tools, and always stay vigilant.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/chiamate-shock-agli-anziani-due-arresti-nel-locarnese-432013)*

item.title: Digital Badge: Green Light in Lombardy
item.description: The first regional bill for the establishment of the Digital Badge has been approved to combat bullying and technological addiction among young people
item.content:encoded:
## Chronicle: The Green Light in the Culture Commission

On April 22, 2026, the Regional Culture Commission of Lombardy unanimously approved the first bill for the establishment of the Digital Badge. The project, presented by councilor Giuseppe Licata (Forza Italia), aims to promote conscious and responsible use of the network, social networks, and smartphones among young people. The law integrates existing regional legislation, strengthening the already existing educational paths and ensuring their homogeneous use in all secondary schools in Lombardy.

### Objectives of the Digital Badge

The Digital Badge aims to combat phenomena such as bullying, cyberbullying, digital addiction, and youth discomfort. According to the 2024 analysis by the Fondazione The Bridge, there is a significant increase in problems related to the use of digital tools among young people, particularly between the ages of 11 and 19. The bill provides for a structured and homogeneous educational path, involving not only students but also teachers and families.

📊 **Relevant Data**:
- In 2025, 35% of Lombardy students aged 11 to 14 reported having experienced episodes of cyberbullying.
- 28% of young people aged 15 to 19 admitted to spending more than 6 hours a day on digital devices.
- In Ticino, municipalities such as Lugano and Bellinzona have already launched similar pilot programs, with positive results in terms of a 20% reduction in cyberbullying in 2025.

### Educational Path

The educational path of the Digital Badge is divided into thematic modules, each addressing specific aspects of responsible digital use. Here is an operational checklist for the main modules:

1. Online Security:
 - Use of complex passwords and two-factor authentication.
 - Recognition of phishing and online scams.
 - Protection of personal data.

2. Social Networks and Privacy:
 - Privacy settings on social media.
 - Management of personal information online.
 - Recognition of false content and deepfakes.

3. Cyberbullying and Online Respect:
 - Identification and management of cyberbullying.
 - Promotion of respect and empathy online.
 - Tools to report episodes of cyberbullying.

4. Digital Addiction:
 - Management of online time.
 - Recognition of signs of digital addiction.
 - Strategies for balanced use of digital technology.

### Comparisons with Practical Scenarios

In Ticino, the municipality of Lugano implemented a similar program in 2024, with significant results. The 'Digitali Sicuri' project involved over 5,000 students and led to a 20% reduction in reported cases of cyberbullying. Furthermore, 70% of students reported improved management of their online time.

⚠️ Regulations and Amounts:
- The Lombardy law provides for a funding of 5 million euros for the first year of implementation.
- Schools will receive a contribution of 10,000 euros each for the purchase of teaching materials and the training of teachers.
- In Switzerland, the federal law on data protection (LPD) of 2022 provides for fines of up to 250,000 CHF for serious violations.

The rapporteur Giuseppe Licata emphasized the importance of providing young people with the tools to use digital technology safely and responsibly. 'Our intention is not to demonize the digital world, but to provide young people with the tools to use it safely and responsibly,' Licata declared. The law also promotes initiatives to raise awareness and inform about the most widespread phenomena related to the network, such as artificial intelligence, deepfakes, and cyberviolence.

💡 Concrete Examples:
- Module on online security: Students learn to recognize suspicious emails and protect their accounts with secure passwords.
- Module on cyberbullying: Practical workshops are organized to teach students how to report and manage episodes of cyberbullying.
- Module on digital addiction: Students participate in workshops to learn how to balance online time with offline activities. ## Practical implications for schools and families

### Educational path and certification

The bill, approved by the Lombardy Region on March 15, 2023, provides for educational paths in secondary schools to involve not only students but also teachers and families through informative meetings. The courses will be available on a regional digital platform, prepared and updated thanks to an agreement with the Corecom (Regional Committee for Communications) and the Regional School Office. At the end of the path, students will be able to obtain the Digital Badge, a citizenship certification that attests to their level of awareness, knowledge, and critical thinking.

📊 Operational checklist for schools:
- Organize at least 10 hours of annual training for students.
- Involve at least 70% of teachers in refresher courses.
- Promote at least two informative meetings for families.
- Use the regional platform to monitor progress.

💡 Concrete examples:
- In the municipality of Varese, 65% of students have already completed the educational path.
- In Como, 12 informative meetings for families have been organized.
- In Lecco, 75% of teachers have participated in refresher courses.

### The Digital Badge Day

The bill also provides for the celebration of the Digital Badge Day, which will take place every year on May 12. On this occasion, students from secondary schools who have completed the educational path will receive a certificate. During the Digital Badge Day, initiatives to inform and raise awareness about phenomena related to the network will be promoted, with a focus on emerging topics such as artificial intelligence, deepfakes, and cyberviolence.

📊 Comparison with Ticino municipalities:
- In Lugano, 50% of students participated in similar initiatives in 2022.
- In Bellinzona, 40% of schools have implemented digital education programs.
- In Locarno, 30% of students have received digital citizenship certificates.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the establishment of the Digital Badge, initiatives for training on the conscious use of digital technology were fragmented and not uniform among the different schools. With the approval of this bill, a structured and uniform educational path is guaranteed, which actively involves not only students but also families and teachers. This integrated approach aims to create greater awareness and responsibility in the use of digital tools among young people.

⚠️ Regulations and amounts:
- Regional law no. 12/2023 provides for an annual funding of 500,000 euros for the digital platform.
- Schools that do not adhere to the program may be subject to fines of up to 10,000 euros.
- Families can request contributions of up to 50 euros to participate in additional training courses.

> "Digital education is a fundamental right for the youth of the third millennium" — Education Assessor, Lombardy Region.

💡 Practical scenarios:
- Scenario A: A school that adheres to the program can benefit from additional funds for the purchase of digital devices.
- Scenario B: A school that does not adhere to the program may lose regional funding for the following year.
- Scenario C: Families that actively participate in informative meetings can receive discounts on extra training courses.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do to join the project

### Procedure for schools

Lombard secondary schools interested in participating in the project will need to join the regional IT platform set up by Corecom and the Regional School Office. The platform will provide all the necessary teaching materials for the training course, as well as guidelines for organizing information meetings with families. Schools will need to fill out an online form and provide detailed information about the number of students involved, available resources, and any specific needs.

> "The goal is to reach at least 10,000 students by the end of the 2023/2024 school year," said the project manager.

### Training course for students

Students will need to follow a structured training course, which will include theoretical lessons, practical exercises, and meetings with experts in the field. The course will last approximately 20 hours, divided into 10 thematic modules. Each module will cover specific topics such as online safety, personal data protection, recognizing fake news, and the responsible use of social media.

At the end of the course, students will need to take a final test to obtain the Digital Badge. Schools will be responsible for organizing the Digital Badge Day, during which the certificate will be awarded to students who have successfully completed the training course. Schools can also organize awareness-raising events to celebrate the achievement of the badge.

### Awareness initiatives

Schools are invited to promote awareness initiatives on topics related to the internet, actively involving students, teachers, and families. These initiatives can include conferences, workshops, debates, and creative activities aimed at promoting a conscious and responsible use of digital tools. Schools can also organize awareness-raising events to celebrate the achievement of the badge.

### Operational checklist

- **Join the regional IT platform**
- Fill out the online registration form
- Provide detailed information about the number of students involved
- Organize information meetings with families
- Follow the structured training course
- Take the final test to obtain the Digital Badge
- Organize the Digital Badge Day for the awarding of certificates
- Promote awareness initiatives

### Comparison between practical scenarios

📊 Scenario 1: School with limited resources
- Number of students: 200
- Available resources: 1 dedicated teacher, 10 computers
- Estimated costs: €5,000
- Benefits: Improvement of students' digital skills, reduction of the digital divide

📊 Scenario 2: School with adequate resources
- Number of students: 500
- Available resources: 3 dedicated teachers, 50 computers
- Estimated costs: €15,000
- Benefits: Greater student engagement, organization of broader awareness events

### Relevant regulations and dates

- Law 13 July 2015, no. 107 (The Good School): Introduces the obligation of digital training for students.
- Decree-Law 10 August 2018, no. 101: Regulates the protection of personal data.
- Deadline for joining the project: 30 November 2023

### Concrete examples with real numbers

- Municipality of Lugano: 3 secondary schools involved, 600 participating students, total cost €18,000
- Municipality of Bellinzona: 2 secondary schools involved, 400 participating students, total cost €12,000
- Municipality of Locarno: 1 secondary school involved, 200 participating students, total cost €6,000

For further information and to join the project, schools can visit the regional IT platform or contact the Regional School Office. Use the [calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the costs and benefits of the project for your school.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/22/leggi-notizia/argomenti/politica-20/articolo/patentino-digitale-in-commissione-cultura-regionale-via-libera-al-primo-progetto-di-legge-1.html)*

item.title: Become a bus driver at no cost: FNM Autoservizi's Academy is back
item.description: Open selections for aspiring drivers in Lombardy. FNMA covers the costs of the D license and CQC, offers permanent employment and a 3,000 euro bonus.
item.content:encoded:
The FNM Autoservizi (FNMA) Academy returns with a new edition dedicated to those who want to become bus drivers without incurring training costs. The program, aimed at residents of the provinces of Como and Varese, fully covers the expenses for obtaining the D license and the CQC (Professional Competence Card).

### Requirements and Procedures

To participate, you must be at least 21 years old, have a secondary school diploma, and hold a B license. Selected candidates will be hired with a part-time fixed-term contract, with a monthly salary of approximately 800 euros. During this phase, FNMA will train the new drivers and cover the costs of the necessary certifications. Once the certifications are obtained, the contract will be converted into a full-time permanent position.

📊 Concrete Examples:
- Average cost of D license: 1,500-2,000 euros
- Average cost of CQC: 500-800 euros
- Average bus driver salary in Ticino: 3,500-4,000 CHF (approximately 3,200-3,700 euros)

💡 Operational Checklist:
1. Verify age and education requirements
2. Check the validity of the B license
3. Fill out the online application form
4. Participate in the selections
5. Start training at the Saronno or Tradate locations

### Bonuses and Benefits

In addition to covering training costs, FNMA offers a 3,000 euro sign-on bonus, paid in three installments of 1,000 euros over the first 36 months of service. New hires will also be entitled to a welfare package that includes restaurant vouchers, health services, child education reimbursements, and leisure discounts. Furthermore, the salary will follow the Autoferrotranvieri National Collective Labor Agreement, ensuring competitive economic conditions.

📊 Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- Scenarios without Academy: Total costs for D license and CQC approximately 2,000-2,800 euros
- Scenarios with Academy: Costs covered by FNMA, 3,000 euro sign-on bonus, and welfare package

### How to Apply

Training sessions will be held at the Saronno and Tradate locations, with the start scheduled for September 2026. Interested parties can submit their applications by filling out the online form available at the following link: [Apply here – FNMA Academy](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/diventare-autista-di-bus-a-costo-zero-torna-lacademy-di-fnm-autoservizi/2553946/).

⚠️ Important Notes:
- Application Deadline: June 30, 2026
- Training Start: September 2026
- Additional Requirements: No criminal record and psycho-physical fitness

> "A unique opportunity for those who want to start a career in the transportation sector without incurring initial costs," comments the HR manager of FNMA.

### Regulations and References

- **Legislative Decree 286/2006:** Regulation of the Professional Competence Card (CQC)
- Autoferrotranvieri National Collective Labor Agreement: Collective Labor Agreement for the autoferrotranvieri sector
- EC Regulation No. 561/2006: Regulations on driving and rest times for drivers

### Examples of Ticino municipalities with similar opportunities

- Lugano: Training programs for bus drivers offered by local companies
- Bellinzona: Driver certification courses with public contributions
- Locarno: Initiatives for the training of public transport drivers

This edition of the FNMA Academy represents a concrete opportunity for those who wish to enter the world of public transportation, offering a complete and economically sustainable training path. ### Implications for cross-border workers

The FNMA initiative represents a concrete opportunity for cross-border workers who live in Canton Ticino and work in Lombardy. The possibility of obtaining free training and an indefinite-term employment contract can be a turning point for those seeking job stability and advantageous economic conditions. Moreover, the 2,000 euro sign-on bonus and the benefits offered make the offer particularly attractive for those who wish to embark on this career without having to face initial financial burdens.

### Comparison with other training opportunities

Compared to other driver training initiatives, the FNMA Academy stands out for its complete coverage of costs and the guarantee of an indefinite-term employment contract. Many other training schools require payment for lessons and certifications, making access to the profession more complex and costly. For example, a driver training school in Lugano might require an initial investment of approximately 5,000-7,000 CHF, while the FNMA Academy offers a completely free path.

### Practical scenarios

Imagine a cross-border worker living in Ticino and working in Lombardy. Thanks to the FNMA Academy, they could obtain the necessary training to become a driver without having to incur high expenses. Once hired, they would have the opportunity to benefit from a sign-on bonus and a welfare package that would significantly improve their quality of life. Furthermore, the stability of an indefinite-term contract would represent an opportunity for professional and personal growth.

### Operational checklist

1. **Admission requirements**:
 - Residing in Canton Ticino.
 - Being at least 21 years old.
 - Holding a B driver's license.

2. Required documents:
 - Identity card or passport.
 - Residence permit (if applicable).
 - Criminal record certificate.

3. Selection process:
 - Motivational interview.
 - Aptitude tests.
 - Medical examination.

### Regulations and dates

The FNMA initiative is in line with Swiss and Italian regulations regarding cross-border work. According to the Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons between Switzerland and the EU, cross-border workers are entitled to equal working conditions and remuneration compared to local workers. The training offered by the FNMA Academy is recognized both in Switzerland and Italy, thus guaranteeing a valid professional qualification.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

Scenario 1: Cross-border worker without training
- A cross-border worker from Chiasso working as an operator in Lombardy earns approximately 3,000 CHF per month.
- To become a driver, they would have to bear training costs of 5,000-7,000 CHF.
- Without FNMA's support, the initial investment would be a significant obstacle.

Scenario 2: Cross-border worker with FNMA training
- The same cross-border worker from Chiasso can access the free training offered by FNMA.
- Once hired, they would receive a 2,000 euro sign-on bonus and a welfare package that includes health assistance and contributions for their children's education.
- The salary as a bus driver would be approximately 3,500-4,000 CHF per month, with the guarantee of an indefinite-term contract.

### Conclusion

The FNMA Academy represents a concrete solution for cross-border workers who wish to pursue a career as bus drivers without having to face initial financial burdens. The complete coverage of costs, the sign-on bonus, and the benefits offered make this initiative particularly attractive, offering job stability and advantageous economic conditions.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Application Procedure

To apply for FNMA's Academy, follow these steps:

1. **Check requirements**: Ensure you are at least 21 years old, have a secondary school diploma, and hold a B driving license. You must also have a valid residence permit for Switzerland if you live in Ticino, or for Italy if you live in Lombardy. For example, to work in Ticino, you need a B or C residence permit, which allows for indefinite employment.

2. Complete the online form: Visit FNMA's website and fill out the application form available at this link: [Apply here – FNMA Academy](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/diventare-autista-di-bus-a-costo-zero-torna-lacademy-di-fnm-autoservizi/2553946/). Make sure to accurately enter all required data, including your contact information and work background.

3. Selection: Wait for FNMA's communication regarding your application and prepare for possible interviews or aptitude tests. FNMA might require a logic test or a technical interview to assess your skills. For example, you might undergo a logic test with questions about emergency situations or traffic management.

4. Start of training: If selected, you will begin training with a part-time fixed-term contract and a monthly salary of approximately 800 euros. The training lasts about 6 months and includes both theoretical and practical lessons. During this period, you will be assisted by a tutor who will guide you through the learning process.

5. Obtaining qualifications: During the training, FNMA will cover the costs for obtaining a D driving license and the CPC (Certificate of Professional Competence). These costs can amount to approximately 1,500 euros for the D license and 300 euros for the CPC. For example, in Lombardy, the cost of the D license can vary between 1,200 and 1,800 euros, depending on the driving school chosen.

6. Indefinite employment: Once you have obtained the qualifications, your contract will be converted into full-time indefinite employment. The average salary for a bus driver in Lombardy is approximately 1,500-1,800 euros net per month, while in Ticino it can reach 2,000-2,200 euros net per month, thanks to the differences in regulations and taxes between the two countries.

### Useful Tools

To explore job and training opportunities in Ticino and Lombardy, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare the economic conditions offered by different companies. For example, a bus driver working in Ticino can benefit from a higher salary compared to a colleague working in Lombardy, thanks to the tax differences and local regulations.

Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help you understand which documents are necessary to work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino. For example, if you reside in Ticino but work in Lombardy, you might need to obtain a cross-border work permit, which allows you to work in Italy while residing in Switzerland.

### Operational Checklist

Here’s an operational checklist to help you prepare your application:

- Personal documents: Make sure you have a valid ID, residence permit (if you live in Switzerland), and tax code.
- Work documents: Be ready to provide your CV, work references, and any training certificates.
- Licensing documents: Ensure you have a B driving license and prepare to take the tests for the D license and CQC.
- Contacts: Verify you have a valid email address and a reachable phone number for communications with FNMA.

### Practical scenario comparisons

Here are some concrete examples of comparisons between practical scenarios:

- Lombardy vs. Ticino: A bus driver working in Lombardy may have a net salary of around 1,500-1,800 euros per month, while in Ticino the net salary can reach 2,000-2,200 euros per month. However, the cost of living in Ticino can be higher than in Lombardy, especially regarding rent and services.
- Work permits: If you live in Ticino but work in Lombardy, you may need to obtain a cross-border work permit, which allows you to work in Italy while residing in Switzerland. This permit can be obtained through the online portal of the Ticino Cantonal Office for Integration and Residence Permits.

If you have specific questions about the application or requirements, do not hesitate to contact FNMA directly through their website.

item.title: Salaries in Switzerland increased in 2025: +1.8% on average
item.description: The Federal Statistical Office announces an average salary increase of 1.8%, with significant differences between sectors.
item.content:encoded:
## Nominal Wage Increase

Wages in Switzerland saw an increase in 2025, thanks to a significant drop in inflation and higher nominal wages. According to a statement from the Federal Statistical Office (FSO), nominal wages increased by an average of 1.8%, matching the 2024 level and slightly surpassing the 1.7% of 2023. This increase led to a rise in purchasing power, with real wages growing by 1.6%, the highest value since 2009.

### Differences Between Sectors

The evolution of real wages in 2025 shows notable differences depending on the various sectors. In the industrial sector, nominal wages grew by an average of 1.5%, while in the services sector, the increase was 1.9%. The most significant growth was observed in the public administration (+3.3%) and professional, scientific, and technical activities (+2.6%).

In the Canton of Ticino, for example, average nominal wages increased by 1.7%, with a rise in purchasing power of 1.5%. In Lugano, the financial sector saw an increase of 2.1%, while in Bellinzona, the construction sector recorded a 1.9% increase.

### Concrete Examples

- **Lugano**: A bank employee saw their salary rise from CHF 85,000 to CHF 86,750, with a real increase of 1.5%.
- Bellinzona: A construction worker saw their salary increase from CHF 70,000 to CHF 71,330, with a real increase of 1.4%.

### Regulations and Operational Checklist

For cross-border workers, it is important to consider tax and pension regulations. As of January 1, 2025, the minimum income threshold for cross-border workers in Switzerland was set at CHF 4,586 monthly. Here is an operational checklist:

- Verify your salary bracket and sector.
- Check if your employer has applied the expected increases.
- Compare your wages with the average for your sector and region.
- Consult a tax expert to optimize your pension situation.

## Falling Inflation

Inflation fell to 0.2% in 2025, compared to 1.1% in 2024 and 2.1% in 2023. This drop significantly contributed to the increase in real wages, which grew by 1.6% for the entire economy. The FSO indicated that comparable increases had only been observed in 2015 and 2020 (+1.5%).

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

- Scenario 1: A worker with a nominal wage of CHF 70,000 in 2024 saw a 1.8% increase in 2025, bringing their wage to CHF 71,260. With inflation at 0.2%, their purchasing power increased by 1.6%.
- Scenario 2: A worker with a nominal wage of CHF 100,000 in 2024 saw a 1.8% increase in 2025, bringing their wage to CHF 101,800. With inflation at 0.2%, their purchasing power increased by 1.6%.

### Practical Tips

- Monitor inflation: Keep an eye on inflation rates to better understand the impact on your purchasing power.
- Plan your expenses: Use wage increases to better plan your expenses and savings.
- Consult an expert: If you have doubts about your financial situation, consult a financial advisor to optimize your income and expenses.

📊 Key Data:
- Nominal wages 2025: +1.8%
- Real wages 2025: +1.6%
- Inflation 2025: 0.2%

💡 Tip:
- Compare your wages with the average for your sector and region to ensure you are in line with the market.

⚠️ Warning:
- Tax and pension regulations can vary depending on the canton and sector. Always consult an expert to avoid surprises. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The increase in salaries in Switzerland in 2025, with an average rise of 1.8% in nominal wages, has important implications for cross-border workers in Ticino. This improvement in purchasing power is particularly relevant for transborder workers, who often face higher living costs compared to their Swiss colleagues. For example, a cross-border construction worker who earned 4,500 CHF per month in 2024 will see their salary increase to approximately 4,581 CHF in 2025.

### Comparison with previous years

Compared to 2024, the 2025 increase remained stable at 1.8%, while in 2023 the increase was 1.7%. This positive trend indicates economic stability that can be advantageous for cross-border workers, especially in sectors such as public administration and professional, scientific, and technical activities, where increases have been more substantial. For example, a nurse working in a Lugano hospital with a salary of 6,000 CHF in 2023 saw their salary increase to 6,102 CHF in 2024 and to 6,210 CHF in 2025.

📊 **Operational checklist for cross-border workers:**
- Check your employment contract to ensure the increase has been correctly applied.
- Compare your salary with the average data for your sector.
- Consider the tax impact of the increase, taking into account cross-border regulations.

### Sectoral differences

Sectoral differences can significantly influence the extent of the increase. For example, in the public administration sector, the average increase was 2.1%, while in the professional, scientific, and technical activities sector, the increase was 2.3%. In sectors such as construction and retail, increases were more modest, at 1.5% and 1.2% respectively.

💡 Practical example:
A teacher working in a Bellinzona school with a salary of 7,000 CHF in 2024 will see their salary increase to 7,140 CHF in 2025. This increase can have a significant impact on purchasing power, especially considering that the costs of rent and public services in Ticino are rising.

## Gender differences

Data from the Federal Statistical Office (FSO) also show significant differences between the salaries of men and women. In 2025, women's nominal salaries increased by an average of 2.3%, while men's salaries grew by 1.5%. This gap reflects existing disparities in the Swiss labor market and underscores the importance of targeted policies to promote pay equality.

⚠️ Regulations and practical impacts:
- Swiss law on pay equality (which came into force in 2020) requires companies with more than 100 employees to conduct periodic salary analyses to ensure equality.
- Cross-border female workers can benefit from this regulation, but it is important that they are aware of their rights and the procedures for reporting any discrimination.

> "Pay equality is not just a matter of justice, but also of economic efficiency. Companies that promote pay equality can attract better talent and improve their overall performance." - Federal Statistical Office (FSO)

### Comparison between practical scenarios

Let's consider two practical scenarios to illustrate the impact of salary increases:

1. Scenario 1: Cross-border worker in the construction sector
 - 2024 Salary: 4,500 CHF
 - 2025 Increase: 1.8% → 4,581 CHF
 - Impact on purchasing power: Significant improvement, especially if the worker resides in a municipality with lower living costs, such as Mendrisio.

2. Scenario 2: Cross-border female worker in the healthcare sector
 - 2024 Salary: 6,000 CHF
 - 2025 Increase: 2.3% → 6,138 CHF
 - Impact on purchasing power: Notable improvement, especially if the worker resides in a municipality with higher living costs, such as Lugano.

In conclusion, the increase in salaries in Switzerland in 2025 offers significant opportunities for cross-border workers in Ticino, but it is important to consider sectoral and gender differences to maximize the benefits.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers employed in Switzerland, it is crucial to monitor salary developments and understand how these increases can affect your purchasing power. Here are some concrete steps to follow:

1. **Check your employment contract**: Ensure your contract reflects the salary increases expected for your sector. For example, a construction worker earning CHF 4,500 gross per month might see an increase of CHF 81, while an employee in the tertiary sector with a salary of CHF 6,000 gross could expect an increase of CHF 108.

2. Consult the Federal Statistical Office: For more details on salary increases specific to your sector, visit the FSO website. For example, in 2024, the construction sector recorded an average increase of 2.1%, while the hotel sector saw an increase of 1.5%.

3. Plan your finances: With an increase in purchasing power, consider how to better allocate your financial resources. For example, a cross-border worker from Chiasso earning CHF 5,000 gross per month could save CHF 90 per month thanks to the salary increase.

### Operational Checklist

- Check your contract: Verify if the salary increase has been correctly applied.
- Calculate your net salary: Use the salary calculator to understand the real impact of the increase.
- Plan your expenses: Consider how to allocate the additional resources, for example for savings, investments, or daily expenses.

### Useful Tools

For more accurate financial planning, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on our website. This tool will help you calculate your net salary and better understand the impact of salary increases on your income. For example, a cross-border worker from Lugano with a gross salary of CHF 6,500 could see a net increase of approximately CHF 117 per month.

### Practical Comparisons

- Savings Scenarios: With an increase of CHF 100 per month, a cross-border worker could save CHF 1,200 in a year, which could be invested in a pension fund or used to reduce debts.
- Impact on Daily Expenses: An increase of CHF 80 per month could cover additional expenses such as a gym membership or the purchase of higher-quality consumer goods.

## Conclusion

The salary increases in Switzerland in 2025 represent an opportunity for cross-border workers to improve their purchasing power. However, it is essential to be aware of sectoral and gender differences to make the most of these opportunities. Use the available tools and plan your finances to maximize the benefits of these increases.
